/*_IMPORTANT: CARNATIC NOTATION REFERENCE DATA - SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
/*_PURPOSE: This file defines ALL valid characters in Carnatic music notation.
VALIDATORS should check against 'validCharacters' section BEFORE parsing.
UPDATE THIS FILE when adding new symbols - validators auto-update from here.
Each song's notation is validated against validCharacters before display.
All note variations (R1, R2, R3 with/without dots) from sruthi_midi_mapping.json
See 'validCharacters.noteVariations' section below for complete symbol reference.

_metadata.description: MIDI Note Octave, Sruthi Mapping, Valid Characters, and All Note Variations Reference
_metadata.usage: Load this file once at page startup. Validators and parsers reference it.
_metadata.defaultSectionDescription: Used as section title when notation appears before any section keyword

plain.example: S R G M P D N

dotsAbove.description: Dots above note → Higher octave C5 (Octave 5) - upper register
dotsAbove.example: Ṡ Ṙ Ġ Ṁ Ṗ Ḋ Ṅ

dotsBelow.description: Dots below note → Lower octave C3 (Octave 3) - lower register
dotsBelow.example: Ṣ Ṛ G̣ Ṃ P̣ Ḍ Ṇ

sruthiMappings.description: For each sruthi, the base note (S/Shadja) maps to the specified semitone. This determines the key signature and transposition of all notes.
sruthiMappings.baseNoteDefinition: S (Shadja) is the fundamental note that defines the sruthi. Other notes are calculated relative to S.

validCharacters./*: VALIDATOR REFERENCE - All characters MUST pass validation before Parse & Display
validCharacters.description: Complete catalog of valid Carnatic music notation symbols and note variations. Validators fetch this data at page load.
validCharacters.validationRule: Before parse and display, check each character in input against these allowed categories. Highlight anything not found in red.

baseSwaras.description: The 7 fundamental notes of Carnatic music

noteVariationsComplete./*: COMPREHENSIVE LIST - All valid note combinations with all variants and octave markers
noteVariationsComplete.description: Each note mapped with all variants (1, 2, 3) and octave markers (plain, dots above, dots below)

Sa_variants.note: S has NO numeric variants. Use dots for octaves only: S, Ṡ (upper), Ṣ (lower)

Pa_variants.note: P has NO numeric variants. Use dots for octaves only: P, Ṗ (upper), P̣ (lower)

,.name: Comma (Rest)
,.usage: Represents a rest for one cell

*.name: Asterisk (Eduppu Marker)
*.usage: Marks where the eduppu (phrase landing) occurs; ignored during note count and rendering; used only as a visual guide

groupingSymbols.description: Symbols for grouping related notes - used for speed modifications and phrase grouping

bracesDetailedDefinition.summary: Curly braces {} denote grouped notes played at 2× speed (double-time notation)
bracesDetailedDefinition.musicMeaning: A notational device to mark phrases or passages that should be performed at double speed relative to the normal beat
bracesDetailedDefinition.syntaxFormat: {notes} where notes can be: base notes (s,r,g,m,p,d,n), variants (r1,r2,etc), rests (,), and separators (|,/,\)
bracesDetailedDefinition.parsingBehavior: Braces are NOT tokenized - they serve as delimiters only
processingPipeline: Step 1: Content extraction - Extract notes/commas from between braces {srgm} → srgm
Step 2: Atom parsing - Parse extracted content into atoms (notes + commas), skipping separators (|,/,\,:,-,.)
Step 3: Pair splitting - Split atoms into pairs, each pair gets unique groupId (e.g., brace-0, brace-2)
Step 4: Flagging - Mark each atom with: speed=2 (double-time), isBraceChild=true, groupId (groups related atoms)

methods.name: tokenizeWithPosition(line, lineNumber)
methods.purpose: Main tokenization function that detects and processes brace groups
methods.logic: Scans for { character, extracts content until }, creates atom objects with speed and groupId flags

methods.name: matchNote(text, i)
methods.purpose: Matches individual note tokens (including variants and combining marks)
methods.logic: Identifies base note, collects numeric suffixes (1,2,3), collects Unicode combining diacriticals

implementation.globalCounter: globalBraceGroupCounter (incremented by 2 for each pair to ensure unique groupIds)

renderingBehavior.display: Atoms with isBraceChild are grouped into cells, typically 2 atoms per cell
renderingBehavior.speedIndicator: A '2×' badge displayed below grouped cells to indicate speed modification
renderingBehavior.cellGrouping: All atoms with same groupId are rendered together in one table cell (see line 1951)

bracesDetailedDefinition.symbolBehavior: Braces are stripped (not rendered) - they appear only in notation source, not in spreadsheet display

metadataKeywords.description: Keywords marking song metadata, sections, and line types. Used by parser to identify structure.
metadataKeywords.parseBehavior: Parser checks lines against these keywords. When found, triggers appropriate parsing behavior.

Composition:.description: Song title - extract value after colon

sectionKeywords.description: Keywords that mark major sections of a composition. Parser creates new section when found. Supports both abbreviated (PALLAV:) and display name (Pallavi:) formats.
sectionKeywords.format: Keyword followed by colon (e.g., PALLAV: or Pallavi:, CHARANAM: or Charanam:)
sectionKeywords.parseBehavior: When encountered: (1) Create new section with matching title, (2) Suppress keyword line from content, (3) Parse subsequent lines as section notation/lyrics

Pallavi:.name: Pallavi
Pallavi:.meaning: First/head section - establishes raga (display name format)

Anupallavi:.name: Anupallavi
Anupallavi:.meaning: Second section - develops theme (display name format)

CHARANAM:.name: Charanam
CHARANAM:.meaning: Third section - culmination, often with multiple sub-sections

Charanam:.name: Charanam
Charanam:.meaning: Third section - culmination, often with multiple sub-sections (display name format)

Charaṇam:.name: Charanam
Charaṇam:.meaning: Third section - culmination with Unicode ṇ (display name format with diacritics)

lineKeywords.description: Keywords that identify line types within sections. Used to label and structure content within sections.
lineKeywords.format: Keyword followed by colon at start of line (e.g., Line:, Line1:, Line2:, LR:)
lineKeywords.parseBehavior: Remove keyword prefix and classify row type based on keyword. Next line inherits classification.

Line:.name: Line
Line:.meaning: Generic notation line (same as Line1:, Line2:, etc.)

Line1:.name: Line 1
Line1:.meaning: First line of a section or subsection

Line2:.name: Line 2
Line2:.meaning: Second line of a section or subsection

LR:.name: Lyric Row
LR:.meaning: Lyric/text corresponding to swara row. LR: must follow a swara line.
LR:.parseInstruction: Extract lyrics and align with previous swara row atoms

infoBlockKeywords.description: Keywords marking information blocks that should be ignored during composition parsing and formatting.
infoBlockKeywords.format: InfoS: (start) and InfoE: (end) as standalone lines
infoBlockKeywords.parseBehavior: All lines between InfoS: and InfoE: (inclusive) are skipped. Not included in song content or atoms.
infoBlockKeywords.useCase: Store metadata, comments, or reference info without affecting parsed composition

InfoS:.name: Info Section Start
InfoS:.meaning: Mark beginning of information block to ignore
InfoS:.action: START SKIP MODE - do not parse lines until InfoE: found

InfoE:.name: Info Section End
InfoE:.action: END SKIP MODE - resume parsing after this line

validCharactersList./*: QUICK REFERENCE - All valid characters as separate lists
validCharactersList.allPlainNotes: S R1 R2 R3 G1 G2 G3 M1 M2 P D1 D2 D3 N1 N2 N3
validCharactersList.allDotsAboveNotes: Ṡ Ṙ Ġ Ṁ Ṗ Ḋ Ṅ
validCharactersList.allDotsBelowNotes: Ṣ Ṛ G̣ Ṃ P̣ Ḍ Ṇ
validCharactersList.allAccentVariants: Ś ś Ŕ ŕ Ɠ ɠ

description: MIDI Note Octave, Sruthi Mapping, Valid Characters, and Complete Note Variations Reference
